counties.name: Archer
counties.assessment: The validated public record shows Archer County discussed repairs and compliance under the Trinity Wind Farm Road Use Agreement, then voted to table action on the compliance issue.

counties.assessment: 3 validated target-specific regulatory/project threads were found. No local restrictive action was validated in the reviewed evidence. Project-facilitation items are tracked separately and do not inflate restrictive risk.

counties.name: Atascosa

counties.name: Austin
counties.assessment: Austin County has an active restrictive policy process for battery storage and data centers, including moratorium or ordinance discussions and public hearings. The supplied records do not validate adoption of a local moratorium, ordinance, or other restriction; they also contain separate public opposition to battery storage.

counties.name: Caldwell
counties.assessment: Caldwell County adopted a state-policy resolution seeking stronger safeguards and local authority, while county materials describe limited existing authority and a negotiated development-agreement strategy. The validated records do not show an adopted county restriction on data centers.

counties.assessment: The records show extensive project-specific solar and battery-storage facilitation through reinvestment zones, tax abatements, road agreements, and related hearings, plus operational monitoring of solar-project traffic. They do not validate an adopted local restriction.

counties.name: Cooke
counties.assessment: Cooke County adopted a resolution urging enhanced state regulation and additional safeguards for data centers. The resolution is state-policy advocacy and does not itself impose a county restriction.

counties.name: Cottle
counties.assessment: The records show solar tax-abatement administration, a proposed cancellation, and general tax-abatement eligibility for solar and data centers. These matters concern incentives rather than siting restrictions, and no adopted local restriction is validated.

counties.name: Crockett
counties.assessment: Crockett County approved a solar tax-abatement amendment after an earlier deferral, and separately allocated prior wind-farm donations. No adopted local restriction is validated.

counties.assessment: Delta County records show solar permits, tax-abatement administration, and a proposed resolution opposing data-center tax abatements. counties.name: Ellis
counties.assessment: Ellis County materials show data-center incentive administration, state-policy advocacy seeking added county authority, and a proposed community-benefit grant. The records do not validate adoption of a county data-center, wind, or solar restriction.

counties.name: Falls
counties.assessment: Falls County ultimately approved a solar road-use agreement after repeated review, while other records concern a separate solar road item, data-center agreement follow-up, and tax litigation. These are facilitation or other material, not an adopted local restriction.

counties.name: Fannin
counties.assessment: Fannin County formed an intergovernmental planning body for battery storage and a zoning commission recommended against issuing a no-permit assurance for a specific project. The record does not show a final countywide restriction or final Commissioners Court disposition of the permit-letter request.

counties.name: Gaines

counties.name: Glasscock
counties.assessment: Glasscock County has existing tax-abatement guidelines that make wind and solar eligible while requiring removal standards and a bond for solar projects. This is an older incentive-policy condition, not a newly adopted general local restriction.

counties.name: Gray

counties.assessment: The validated public record shows Kimble County discussed three potential wind farms, a possible resolution, road-use agreements, bond requirements, and easement issues, and deferred action for further legal discussion.

counties.name: Kinney

counties.name: Knox
counties.assessment: No adopted local restriction is validated.
